As a beginner in trading, keep these four principles firmly in mind:
1. Think long-term. Real success in trading is built over time, not overnight.
2. Be prepared to take calculated risks. Every investment carries risk, so learn to manage it wisely.
3. Be disciplined with your money. If your spending is not helping you grow or generate returns, reconsider it.
4. Never assume you know everything. Stay humble, keep learning, ask questions, and seek guidance from a qualified financial advisor whenever possible.

😂 if trading was by reading a course and opening the chart to earn everyone trading would have been rich, tbh it isn't. You have to treat this shit like sport getting up each day and studying the chart for about 3hr + to be good at it and learning from previous history of the candle sticks and technical patterns blueprint
In this chart for the first example I see FVG's before the demand zone... which do I pay attention to more FVG or demand zones... (essentially which one takes more priority)

Daytrading works, just not at scale. Up to 7 figures, you can do well (even then I'll say switch to swing trading). Swing trading and long-term investing is the approach once you start to scale. Buying stock with little money almost never makes sense. If we both take $10k and you invest it in a stock that performs 50% for the year, I'll hit that 50% in a week trading indices. Efficiency is the only way to true wealth.

For all the new traders out there: stop staring at the 1-minute charts. I burned so much money early on chasing every little price wiggle and fakeout. The game changed for me when I switched to higher timeframes and started focusing on institutional order flow. My stress disappeared and my winning trades finally started hitting consistently. 

Trading is 90% patience and 10% execution. Master the wait.

Where do you go to trade???? I’m new literally started last week

For beginners 
1). Trade or invest only in uptrend stock which are trading above 100,200,500 moving Average 
2). Check if company is making money in Quarterly report 
3). Enter with small amount so incase if market go down you will be able to average 
4). Be willing to take risk
5). Never claim to know - Ask questions and it's best you work with a financial advisor
6). Don`t start from intraday or options or futures, just start from investing 
Good luck .

I started trading to pay bills. 
No other source of income.
Do or die. 
Now I make about $400-$500 per day on small pre-market moves. Yes, I trade the trend on the 5 minute chart using 9 EMA and VWAP. I learned the hard way how to take a small profit. It’s ok to close a small trade and be done for the day. it’s all about the psychology. When I stopped trying to turn a $300 trade into a $1,000 trade, I started winning.
Noteworthy!

Great video! I really do have a question. For someone with less than $10,000 to invest, how would you recommend we enter the crypto market? I am looking at studying some traders and copying their strategy rather than investing myself and losing money emotionally. What’s your take on this approach?

1. have winning bigger than your losses.
2. have filters
3. think in probabilities and not in certainty
4. take breaks instead of quitting 
5. create a strategy that fits your personality
6. don't chase
7. work on yourself
8. make sure you understand the bigger picture
9.ask for help
10. get enough sleep,
10minutes well spent!
